completedWork: Superposition Theorem Simulator - NEW: Interactive simulation demonstrating the superposition theorem for linear circuits with multiple independent sources. Features: Dual voltage source configuration (V1 and V2, 1-50V each), enable/disable individual sources, three adjustable resistors (R1, R2, R3: 1-100Ω). Real-time calculations: Node voltages (A, B, C), branch currents through each resistor, individual source contributions (I1', I2'), total current via algebraic summation (I = I1' + I2'), power dissipation per resistor. Visual feedback: Interactive SVG circuit diagram with animated current flow, color-coded source contributions (blue for V1, green for V2), current contribution bar charts showing individual and total currents, real-time node voltage and branch current displays, step-by-step superposition analysis panel. Quick presets: Equal Sources (12V each), Opposing (12V vs 12V with mismatched resistors), V1 Dominant (24V vs 6V), V2 Dominant (6V vs 24V). Educational content: Superposition theorem definition and principles, when to use the theorem, key limitations (linear circuits only, no direct power calculation), comparison with other analysis methods. File: pages/simulations/superposition-theorem.vue

Maximum Power Transfer Calculator - NEW: Calculate and analyze maximum power transfer theorem with interactive visualization. Features: Source voltage control (1-100V), source/internal resistance (1-1000Ω), load resistance adjustment (1-1000Ω) with optimal load indicator. Real-time calculations: Load power (Pₗ = Vₛ² × Rₗ / (Rₛ + Rₗ)²), load voltage and current, efficiency (η = Rₗ / (Rₛ + Rₗ) × 100%), source power and losses, total circuit resistance, maximum possible power (at Rₗ = Rₛ). Visual feedback: Interactive SVG circuit diagram with animated current flow, Power vs Load Resistance curve showing operating point and max power point, Efficiency vs Load Resistance curve (highlighting 50% at max power), status indicator (optimal/near optimal/suboptimal), real-time result cards with percentage of maximum power. Quick presets: Audio Amp (10V, 8Ω), Battery (12V, 0.1Ω internal), Antenna (5V, 50Ω), Solar Panel (18V, 5Ω). Quick action button to instantly set Rₗ = Rₛ for maximum power transfer. Educational content: Maximum Power Transfer theorem statement, key formulas (Pₗ(max) = Vₛ² / (4 × Rₛ)), insights about power vs efficiency trade-off (50% efficiency at max power), applications in audio systems, RF circuits, battery charging, and solar panels with MPPT. File: pages/calculators/maximum-power-transfer.vue

Navigation Updates - Added Superposition Theorem simulation to pages/simulations/index.vue with interactive preview card showing circuit diagram with dual sources. Added Maximum Power Transfer calculator to pages/calculators/index.vue with id=81, categorized under 'basic', 'dc-circuits', and 'power' categories.
